title: psathyrostachys rupestris
intro: Psathyrostachys rupestris, also known as Tibetan wheat grass or Tibetan grass, is a type of grass native to the high-altitude regions of the Tibetan Plateau. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and enhancing immune function. In herbal preparations, Psathyrostachys rupestris is often combined with other plants to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
